task: reasoning through our paper, how likely is it an accepted one into neurips 2026

plan strategic fixes for: "  Vulnerabilities (what keeps you out)

  1. "The fix is trivially obvious" — Once you diagnose the problem, "just route on raw input" is a
  one-line fix. Reviewers who focus on method novelty (vs. diagnosis novelty) will score 4-5 here.
  2. DLinear gap + Raw-MLP MoE — The TSFM backbone contributes ~0.4% over a raw MLP ensemble. A
  skeptical reviewer asks: "Why bother with a foundation model at all?" The deployment-regime
  argument is reasonable but not a slam-dunk.
  3. Proposition 2 is MI chain rule — A theory-focused reviewer will note that the core math is
  standard information theory, not a novel bound. The hand-wavy Part (iv) constant weakens this
  further.
  4. Page compliance — At ~2057 lines, the main body may still be tight. Desk rejection risk is real
   if the body spills past 9 pages.". Design and run more experiments if necessary. We had GPU at our Race VM (recall from memory), keep GPUs busy
